articles.title: Harvard scientists turn a silicon chip into a DNA writing machine
articles.summary: Scientists have created a silicon chip that can write dozens of DNA sequences simultaneously using electricity and water-based enzymes, offering a cleaner alternative to conventional DNA manufacturing. The breakthrough…

articles.title: New 3D COF structure could help tune porous materials for batteries and cleanup

articles.title: Special relativity can warp chemical bonds – now we've seen it happen
articles.summary: An experiment with a charged molecule of bismuth and carbon reveals how effects from Albert Einstein’s special relativity reshape the standard understanding of chemical bonds

articles.title: Physicists finally build a quantum material predicted more than a decade ago

articles.title: NASA Sets Coverage for Astronaut Anil Menon Launch to Space Station

articles.title: SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket launches for 35th time, hauls Starlink satellites to orbit (video)
articles.summary: A SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket flew for the 35th time tonight (July 10), carrying 29 Starlink satellites to low Earth orbit.

articles.title: SpaceX wants to launch 100,000 Starlink satellites to orbit
articles.summary: SpaceX has applied for approval to operate a 100,000-member megaconstellation of next-gen Starlink satellites in Earth orbit. And each of the new spacecraft will weigh about 4,400 pounds.

articles.title: Japanese company books 1,100 pounds of cargo space on SpaceX Starship mission to the moon
articles.summary: The Japanese company ispace is expanding its already extensive moon plans to include SpaceX's Starship megarocket.

articles.title: Scientists warn invasive Asian mantises are threatening Europe's wildlife
articles.summary: Two striking Asian praying mantis species that have rapidly spread across Europe have now been officially classified as invasive, raising new concerns about their impact on native wildlife. Boosted by climate change and…

articles.title: Rare fossil goose rewrites the story of New Zealand's giant birds
articles.summary: Scientists have discovered a previously unknown fossil goose that challenges a decades-old theory about the evolution of New Zealand's birds. The find suggests the country's giant flightless geese evolved from much more…

articles.title: A worm that lived half a billion years ago preferred turning right
articles.summary: Fossils of Spriggina floundersi provide the earliest evidence of animals favouring one side of the body over the other – a feature of nervous systems that we see in our own right- and left-handedness

articles.title: Brutal heat wave forecast for western US this weekend

articles.title: Where Venezuela’s Earthquakes Shifted the Ground
articles.summary: Radar data from the NISAR satellite show that La Guaira and nearby areas experienced significant ground displacement from the June 2026 temblors.

articles.title: The total solar eclipse of August 12, 2026
articles.summary: On August 12, 2026, a total solar eclipse will be visible parts of Arctic, Greenland, Iceland, and Spain. Details here. The post The total solar eclipse of August 12, 2026 first appeared on EarthSky .

articles.title: Europe's most active volcano may have a secret origin
articles.summary: Mount Etna has long puzzled geologists because it doesn't fit any of the three classic ways volcanoes are thought to form. A new study suggests it may instead be fueled by ancient pockets of magma that are pushed upward…

articles.title: Thousands shelter in Taiwan as typhoon lashes Japan islands
articles.summary: More than 14,000 people in Taiwan have fled their homes, and many shops remain closed, as a typhoon pounding Japan's remote southwestern islands swept toward China on Saturday.

articles.title: Mathematicians put AI to work on Fermat's last theorem
articles.summary: At an event in London, mathematicians have made unexpectedly fast progress on formalising Fermat's last theorem using AI

articles.title: Mathematics of thermodynamics is being rewritten after 200 years
articles.summary: The laws of physics that concern heat and work could gain a firmer mathematical footing thanks to “gauge theory”, which already helps us understand quantum fields

articles.title: Heidelberg physicists just united two opposing quantum theories

articles.title: What is 'social media'? Study highlights public confusion about the term
articles.summary: Research from Aston University has shown that the public has no clearly accepted definition for the term "social media" or agreement about which websites and platforms are classified as such.

articles.title: A surprisingly detailed look at the physics of a lugworm's poop
articles.summary: Feedback is delighted by a study of how many animals produce poop that echoes the look of the poop emoji – even the lugworm, which does it upside down

articles.title: 5 graphs that show how heatwaves are getting more dangerous
articles.summary: Longer-lasting hot spells and high temperatures at night are making it harder to cope, leading to thousands more deaths from extreme heat
